title: Frontier final-assembly: large-memory / out-of-core consolidation strategy
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The single-host final assembly is the record-scale bottleneck; this backlog task designs a large-memory / out-of-core consolidation strategy to push past it while preserving the determinism and verification contracts.
objective: Design a large-memory / out-of-core final-assembly consolidation strategy that addresses the single-host bottleneck at record scale.
acceptanceCriteria: Designs an out-of-core / large-memory final-assembly consolidation strategy that addresses the single-host bottleneck at record scale.
Documents memory/time tradeoffs and a reproducible plan; preserves the determinism and verification contracts; CC-BY-4.0.
output: A document describing the frontier large-memory/out-of-core final-assembly consolidation strategy.
